questions.question: What is a fractal?
questions.answer: A fractal is a geometric pattern that repeats itself at different scales.

questions.question: What is self-similarity?
questions.answer: Self-similarity means a shape looks similar at different magnifications.

questions.question: Give one example of a fractal.
questions.answer: Sierpinski Triangle.

questions.question: Which shape is used to start the Sierpinski Triangle?
questions.answer: An equilateral triangle.

questions.question: Which shape is used to start the Sierpinski Carpet?
questions.answer: A square.

questions.question: What is the Koch Snowflake?
questions.answer: A fractal formed by repeatedly modifying the sides of an equilateral triangle.

questions.question: What is a polyhedron?
questions.answer: A polyhedron is a solid with flat polygonal faces.

questions.question: What are faces of a solid?
questions.answer: Faces are the flat surfaces of a solid.

questions.question: What are edges of a solid?
questions.answer: Edges are line segments where two faces meet.

questions.question: What are vertices of a solid?
questions.answer: Vertices are points where edges meet.

questions.question: How many faces does a cube have?
questions.answer: A cube has 6 faces.

questions.question: How many edges does a cube have?
questions.answer: A cube has 12 edges.

questions.question: How many vertices does a cube have?
questions.answer: A cube has 8 vertices.

questions.question: What is a net of a solid?
questions.answer: A net is a flat pattern that can be folded to form a solid.

questions.question: How many nets does a cube have?
questions.answer: A cube has 11 nets.

questions.question: What is projection in geometry?
questions.answer: Projection is the representation of a three-dimensional object on a two-dimensional plane.

questions.question: What is meant by front view?
questions.answer: Front view shows how an object appears from the front.

questions.question: What is meant by top view?
questions.answer: Top view shows how an object appears from above.

questions.question: What is meant by side view?
questions.answer: Side view shows how an object appears from the side.

questions.question: What is an isometric drawing?
questions.answer: An isometric drawing represents a 3D object on a 2D plane using equal angles.

questions.question: What is an isometric grid?
questions.answer: An isometric grid is a pattern of lines used to draw 3D objects accurately.

questions.question: How does a cube appear in isometric projection?
questions.answer: It appears as a hexagon outline.

questions.question: What does a shadow represent in geometry?
questions.answer: A shadow represents the projection of an object.

questions.question: Where are fractals used in real life?
questions.answer: Fractals are used in computer graphics and modeling natural patterns.

questions.question: Why are projections important in engineering?
questions.answer: They help represent 3D objects clearly on 2D drawings.
